commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram group dispatch so normal-group tool/progress summaries are delivered when verbose state or defaults are on, adds focused dispatch tests, and documents the group `/verbose` behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level for the central issue: current main suppresses normal Telegram group tool/progress summaries, and the PR body includes redacted before/after live Telegram transcripts for `/verbose off` and `/verbose on`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: There is one narrow code defect automation can repair; after that, maintainers still need to decide the Telegram group disclosure policy.

target.title: fix(telegram): honor /verbose in groups
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted with Codex; I reviewed the diff, ran live Telegram proof, targeted tests, and local Codex review.

- Problem: normal Telegram groups suppress text-only tool/progress summaries even when `/verbose on` is enabled.
- Solution: allow normal Telegram groups to send tool/progress summaries when the existing verbose session state is enabled.
- What changed: Telegram group dispatch now treats `/verbose on` as the signal to allow visible tool/progress summaries in normal groups.
- What did NOT change: `/verbose off` keeps summaries hidden; Telegram forum topic behavior and Slack non-DM behavior are unchanged.

## Motivation

Private Telegram QA groups are useful for testing real OpenClaw behavior, but without visible tool/progress summaries it is hard to verify what the agent actually did in Telegram. `/verbose` already controls this visibility elsewhere, so normal Telegram groups should honor the same session state.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  Normal Telegram groups did not show text-only tool/progress summaries after `/verbose on`.

- Real environment tested:
  Private Telegram QA group with two BotFather bots:
  - Driver bot sends prompts into the group.
  - SUT bot runs OpenClaw gateway from this branch.
  - Normal Telegram supergroup, not a forum topic.
  - Tokens, chat IDs, and private config are omitted.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Start QA SUT gateway from this branch.
  2. Send `/verbose@qa_sut_bot off`.
  3. Prompt SUT: `Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-fix-verbose-off-done.`
  4. Send `/verbose@qa_sut_bot on`.
  5. Prompt SUT: `Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-fix-verbose-on-done.`

- Evidence after fix:
  Copied live Telegram transcript from the QA group:

  ```text
  /verbose@qa_sut_bot off
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging disabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-fix-verbose-off-done.
  QA SUT: qa-fix-verbose-off-done

  /verbose@qa_sut_bot on
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging enabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-fix-verbose-on-done.
  QA SUT: 🛠️ pwd
  QA SUT: qa-fix-verbose-on-done
  ```

  Screenshot can be attached showing the same QA Driver prompt, visible `🛠️ pwd` tool summary, and final SUT response.

- Observed result after fix:
  `/verbose off` keeps normal Telegram group tool summaries hidden. `/verbose on` shows the `🛠️ pwd` tool summary before the final reply.

- What was not tested:
  I did not run the full test suite on this low-memory VPS. I did not test Slack live. I did not test a live Telegram forum topic because existing focused tests cover the unchanged forum-topic path.

- Before evidence:
  Copied live Telegram transcript from `origin/main`:

  ```text
  /verbose@qa_sut_bot off
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging disabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-main-verbose-off-done.
  QA SUT: qa-main-verbose-off-done

  /verbose@qa_sut_bot on
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging enabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-main-verbose-on-done.
  QA SUT: qa-main-verbose-on-done
  ```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause:
  Telegram normal-group dispatch allowed verbose/tool summaries only outside normal groups, or in forum-topic groups. It did not consider the existing `/verbose` session state for normal Telegram groups.

- Missing detection / guardrail:
  There was focused coverage for suppressing normal group tool summaries and for allowing forum-topic summaries, but not for `/verbose on` enabling summaries in normal Telegram groups.

- Contributing context:
  `/verbose` already controls tool/progress summary visibility in other Telegram contexts.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient

- Target test or file:
  `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`

- Scenario the test should lock in:
  Normal Telegram groups should suppress tool summaries when verbose is off, and show them when verbose is on. Forum-topic behavior should remain unchanged.

- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  The behavior is controlled in dispatch gating, so focused dispatch tests directly cover the regression without requiring a live Telegram dependency.

- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
  Existing tests covered group suppression and forum-topic delivery, but not normal group delivery controlled by verbose state.

- If no new test is added, why not:
  N/A. New focused tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`/verbose on` now shows tool/progress summaries in normal Telegram groups. `/verbose off` keeps those summaries hidden.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Telegram group + /verbose on -> tool summary suppressed -> final reply only

After:
Telegram group + /verbose off -> final reply only
Telegram group + /verbose on  -> tool summary -> final reply
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No execution behavior changed; existing verbose tool/progress summaries become visible in normal Telegram groups only when `/verbose on` is active.
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  Risk: visible tool/progress summaries can expose command names or arguments to group members. Mitigation: normal groups remain quiet by default when `/verbose off`; users must enable `/verbose on` for that group session.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux VPS
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw gateway running from this branch
- Model/provider: existing QA SUT config
- Integration/channel: Telegram normal group
- Relevant config (redacted): private QA Telegram group with Driver bot and SUT bot; tokens and chat IDs omitted

### Steps

1. Start the QA SUT gateway from this branch.
2. In the QA Telegram group, send `/verbose@qa_sut_bot off`.
3. Prompt the SUT to run `pwd` and reply with a marker.
4. Confirm only the final marker appears.
5. Send `/verbose@qa_sut_bot on`.
6. Prompt the SUT to run `pwd` and reply with a marker.
7. Confirm Telegram shows `🛠️ pwd`, then the final marker.

### Expected

Normal Telegram group tool/progress summaries are hidden with `/verbose off` and visible with `/verbose on`.

### Actual

Matches expected.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Local Codex review:
  - Command: `codex review --base origin/main`
  - Reviewed head: `2538280e1a82dcaca49070ad5d31c9e1c938b1bd`
  - Result: no actionable regressions found.
  - Codex also ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`, which passed: `141 passed`.

- Verified scenarios:
  - `origin/main`: `/verbose on` still produced final reply only in normal Telegram group.
  - This branch: `/verbose off` produced final reply only.
  - This branch: `/verbose on` produced visible `🛠️ pwd` tool summary, then final reply.

- Edge cases checked:
  - Existing forum-topic behavior remains covered by focused dispatch test.
  - Slack non-DM suppression remains unchanged by the dispatch condition.

- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full repository test suite.
  - Live Slack behavior.
  - Live Telegram forum topic behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `/verbose on` in a normal Telegram group can expose tool/progress summary text to group members.
  - Mitigation: `/verbose off` remains the default quiet behavior for normal groups, and this PR does not add a new always-on config flag.
target.author: kurplunkin
